title: wilson accepts invalid z values that can silently corrupt intervals
reasoning: The public API exposes z as a caller-supplied normal quantile, and the function explicitly fails loud for invalid k/n to avoid NaN-poisoned safety-gate decisions. However, z is not validated before it is used in interval arithmetic. A non-finite z produces NaN bounds, while a negative z can invert the half-width and violate the expected lo <= p <= hi invariant. The validation tests cover invalid k/n inputs but not invalid z, so a bad configured quantile can still produce wrong-but-plausible gate data instead of failing loud.
reproduction: Call wilson(5, 10, Number.NaN) and observe NaN interval fields, or wilson(5, 10, -1.96) and observe inverted bounds relative to p.
recommendation: Validate z in wilson, for example require Number.isFinite(z) && z >= 0 or z > 0 depending on the intended confidence-interval contract, and throw RangeError on invalid values before computing z2.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The invalid-input suite only asserts RangeError for k > n, negative k, non-integer n, and NaN k; it never passes an invalid custom z.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add cases asserting wilson(5, 10, Number.NaN), wilson(5, 10, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY), and wilson(5, 10, -1.96) throw RangeError, plus one valid custom z case if custom quantiles are supported.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists. Current packages/stats/src/main/stats.ts now documents z validation at lines 35-38 and enforces it at lines 44-46 with !Number.isFinite(z) || z < 0 throwing RangeError before interval arithmetic. Current packages/stats/src/test/stats.test.ts still has the original invalid k/n tests at lines 253-258 and now adds regression coverage at lines 260-264 for Number.NaN, POSITIVE_INFINITY, and -1.96 throwing RangeError, while allowing z=0. I attempted to run the stats test suite, but the read-only sandbox prevented Vitest/Vite from creating temporary files, first under node_modules/.vite-temp and then under /var/folders. Based on current implementation plus checked-in regression tests, the reported issue is fixed.
commands: pwd && rg -n "function wilson|export function wilson|wilson\(" packages/stats/src/main/stats.ts packages/stats/src/test/stats.test.ts
rg -n "describe\(\"input validation|invalid custom z|finite non-negative z|Number\.isFinite\(z\)|z < 0" packages/stats/src/main/stats.ts packages/stats/src/test/stats.test.ts
pnpm --filter @codenuke/stats exec vitest run --configLoader runner packages/stats/src/test/stats.test.ts
pnpm --filter @codenuke/stats exec vitest run --configLoader runner src/test/stats.test.ts
